title: Ref assignment and element registry
description: Create src/snapshot/refs.ts that assigns stable ref IDs to interactive elements. Maintain a session-scoped registry mapping refs to CDP node IDs or selectors. Refs should be short (e1, e2...) and stable across snapshots of the same page state.

title: Add string length limits to act command inputs
description: The act command accepts unbounded string inputs (text for typing, fn for evaluate, values for select). Add reasonable upper bounds to prevent memory issues: text max 1MB, fn max 100KB, individual values max 10KB.
close_reason: Added length limits: text=1MB, fn=100KB, values=10KB

title: OpenClaw configuration schema and resolution
description: Add optional 'openclaw' section to config schema: enabled, url, profile, snapshotFormat, refs, efficient. Support env vars SWEETLINK_OPENCLAW_URL and SWEETLINK_OPENCLAW_PROFILE. Defaults: url=http://127.0.0.1:18791, snapshotFormat=ai, refs=role, efficient=false.

title: CLI command: sweetlink ai-snapshot
description: Add 'sweetlink ai-snapshot <session>' command that captures an AI-optimized snapshot of the page. Options: --interactive (only interactive elements), --depth <n>, --compact. Uses the native accessibility tree, not OpenClaw.

title: Fix race condition in health cache
description: The health cache in OpenClawClient has a potential race condition where multiple concurrent calls to health() could trigger multiple fetches before the cache is populated. Fix by using promise-based synchronization (cache the pending promise, not just the result).
close_reason: Implemented promise-based deduplication in health() method

title: Native AI snapshots and ref-based actions
description:
Implement OpenClaw-like functionality natively in SweetLink using CDP/Puppeteer. Provides AI-optimized page snapshots and ref-based element interactions without requiring a separate OpenClaw server.

Goals:
- Traverse browser accessibility tree via CDP
- Generate concise, AI-readable snapshots with element refs
- Support ref-based click/type/hover actions
- Optional: labels overlay on screenshots

This is an alternative to the OpenClaw backend integration (PR #4).
close_reason: All child tasks completed: accessibility tree, refs, formatter, CLI commands, tests

title: AI snapshot formatter
description: Create src/snapshot/formatter.ts that converts accessibility tree + refs into concise text output. Format: 'role "name" [ref:id]' with indentation for hierarchy. Support options: interactive-only, max-depth, compact mode.

title: OpenClaw HTTP client module
description: Create src/openclaw/client.ts that wraps HTTP calls to OpenClaw browser control server. Endpoints: GET /snapshot, POST /act, POST /screenshot, POST /navigate, GET /tabs, POST /tabs/open, POST /tabs/focus, DELETE /tabs/:targetId, POST /pdf, POST /hooks/dialog, POST /hooks/file-chooser. Health check (GET /?profile=<profile>), connection retry, 5s health cache, error mapping from HTTP status codes to typed errors.

title: CLI commands: snapshot, act, navigate (OpenClaw delegation)
description: Add/extend CLI commands to delegate to OpenClaw when enabled: 'sweetlink snapshot [session] [--format ai|aria] [--efficient] [--interactive] [--labels]', 'sweetlink act [session] --kind <kind> --ref <ref>' (new command supporting click/type/press/hover/drag/select/fill/resize/wait/evaluate/close), 'sweetlink navigate [session] <url>', 'sweetlink screenshot' extended with --ref and --full-page. Use Commander patterns matching existing commands.

title: Tests for OpenClaw integration
description: Unit tests for OpenClaw client (mock HTTP responses for all endpoints, error codes, health cache). Integration tests gated behind SWEETLINK_OPENCLAW_TEST=1 env var that hit a real OpenClaw instance. Use vitest patterns matching existing test suite.

title: Tests for native AI snapshot
description: Unit tests for accessibility tree parsing, ref assignment, and snapshot formatting. Integration tests using JSDOM or a real browser session. Mock CDP responses for unit tests.
close_reason: Added 47 unit tests for accessibility, refs, and formatter modules

title: Ref-based actions: click, type, hover
description: Extend existing click/type commands or add new ref-based variants. Given a ref ID from a snapshot, resolve it to an element and perform the action. Use the ref registry to map refs back to CDP nodes.
close_reason: Implemented ai-act command for ref-based click/type/hover/etc

title: Browser backend abstraction layer
description: Create a browser backend interface that both the existing Puppeteer/CDP backend and the new OpenClaw backend implement. Operations: snapshot, act, screenshot, navigate, tabs, pdf, dialog, fileUpload. Factory function selects backend based on config. Graceful fallback from OpenClaw to native when OpenClaw is unavailable.

title: Add fetch timeout to OpenClaw HTTP requests
description: HTTP requests to the OpenClaw server have no timeout, which could cause the CLI to hang indefinitely if the server is unresponsive. Add configurable timeout (default 30s) using AbortController.
close_reason: Added AbortController-based 30s timeout to all HTTP requests

title: Fallback logic: OpenClaw → native Puppeteer/CDP
description: When OpenClaw is unreachable or returns an error: 1) Log error with context, 2) Fall back to SweetLink native Puppeteer/CDP for that operation, 3) Emit warning suggesting user check 'openclaw browser status'. Graceful degradation per-operation, not per-session.

title: CDP accessibility tree module
description: Create src/snapshot/accessibility.ts that fetches the full accessibility tree via CDP Accessibility.getFullAXTree. Parse nodes, extract roles, names, values, and interactive states. Handle iframes and shadow DOM where possible.
close_reason: Implemented CDP accessibility tree module in src/snapshot/accessibility.ts

title: OpenClaw status command and health check
description: 'sweetlink openclaw status' command that checks connectivity to OpenClaw browser control server via GET /?profile=<profile>. Report running state, cdpReady, profile info. Cache health for 5s. Integrate into existing status/health reporting.
